Gerard Salton papers., Undated
Collection Number: 16-13-2908

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Gerard Salton papers., Undated
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
16-13-2908
Abstract:
Subject files, correspondence, class notebooks, videocassettes, reprints, and other papers and records deriving from Salton's work in text processing, information retrieval, and computer science.
Creator:
Salton, Gerard.
Quanitities:
110 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

Professor of Computer Science, Cornell University. Salton has worked in the areas of natural language text processing, automatic text processing, and informational retrieval; editor-in-chief, Association for Computing Machinery Communications; Guggenheim Fellow, 1963.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Subject files, correspondence, class notebooks, videocassettes, reprints, and other papers and records deriving from Salton's work in text processing, information retrieval, and computer science.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Gerard Salton papers. #16-13-2908. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:

Access to Boxes 91-96 restricted to the permission of the University Archivist.
SUBJECTS

Names:
Association for Computing Machinery
Subjects:
Information science -- Study and teaching.
Computers -- Study and teaching.
Information storage and retrieval systems.
Text processing (computer science).
Computer science -- Study and teaching. -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.
Form and Genre Terms:
Videotapes.

Box 89
30 Years of Information Retrieval at Cornell. A SMART Celebration. Tapes 1, 2, and 3 (Master Copy)
 April 22, 1995
Scope and Contents
Contains talks by Chris Buckley, Robert Constable, Donna Harman, Michael McGill, David Evans, Michael Lesk, Edward Fox, Karen Sparck Jones, Keith van Rijsbergen, William Cooper, W. Bruce Croft, and Gerard Salton.
